meta_description: Learn how to API integration guide and overcome can't debug code. A Beginner-level Case Study using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Freelancers: API Integration Guide to Solve Debugging Woes
content.seo_description: Learn how to master API integration and overcome debugging roadblocks. A beginner-level case study using Antigravity and Claude Code for faster shipping.
content.answer_block: Debugging API failures is usually a breakdown in schema validation or request headers. By leveraging Antigravity for architectural scaffolding and Claude Code for real-time trace analysis, you can automate error detection. This workflow allows you to identify payload mismatches and authentication issues in seconds, not hours, ensuring rapid project delivery.
content.intro:
The clock is ticking. Your client is expecting a live demo in three hours, but your API integration is throwing a cryptic 400 Bad Request error. You’re staring at a wall of logs, feeling the familiar weight of 'can't debug code' anxiety. It’s the bottleneck that separates the amateurs from the high-earning freelancers.

But here is the secret: you don't need to be a debugger; you need to be an architect. By treating API integration as a structured, automated process rather than a manual chase, you can turn these roadblocks into routine tasks. We’re going to walk through a battle-tested workflow using Antigravity and Claude Code to eliminate the guesswork.

steps.title: Define API Schema with Antigravity
steps.description: Before writing code, generate a strict schema to prevent integration errors. This ensures your data structures match the provider's expectations.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze this API documentation [URL] and generate a TypeScript interface and Zod validation schema for the primary resource.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'generate a schema.ts file based on the provided API docs'

steps.title: Initialize Request Client
steps.description: Use a robust client setup to handle retries and logging automatically, reducing the surface area for bugs.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create an Axios instance with interceptors for logging requests and responses, and add a retry mechanism for 5xx errors.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'create api-client.ts with axios and retry logic'

steps.title: Automated Error Trace
steps.description: When an error occurs, use Claude Code to parse the raw log file directly to identify the exact line of failure.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Review the following error logs and identify if the issue is a payload mismatch, authentication failure, or network timeout.
steps.claude_code_command: claude ask 'Analyze the error log in ./logs/api-error.log and explain why the request failed'

steps.title: Unit Test Integration
steps.description: Write a mock test to verify the integration without hitting the live API, ensuring your logic is sound.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a Vitest suite for this API service that mocks the network response and validates the Zod schema.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'create api.test.ts to test the integration logic'

steps.title: Final Integration Polish
steps.description: Deploy the integration and use Claude Code to perform a final sanity check on the live environment.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Review the integrated code for potential security vulnerabilities and performance bottlenecks.
steps.claude_code_command: claude ask 'Check the current codebase for any security issues or inefficient API calls'

pro_tips: Always log the full request body and response header in development; never guess what the API sent back.
Use Zod for runtime type checking so you catch schema violations before they reach your database.
Implement a 'Circuit Breaker' pattern to prevent your app from crashing during API downtime.
Store API keys in environment variables and use a .env.example file to avoid accidental credential leakage.
When you hit a '403 Forbidden', check the timestamp on your server; clock skew is a silent killer of API authentication.
content.next_steps: Integrate these patterns into your base project template so you never have to manually setup an API client again.
content.conversion_block: Stop wasting hours hunting for missing commas and malformed JSON. Master the tools that make you look like a senior engineer and deliver projects that wow your clients. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
